titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/230

respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=188 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:20:00.435031+00 | translation moa=189 status=live modified=2021-04-05 09:20:01.812941+00

p.cl: Rodbertus dei gratia sancti Andree episcopus . Omnibus sancte matris ęcclesię filiis  salutem  benedictionem .

Sciant tam posteri quam presentes me concessisse in perpe
tuam elemosinam deo  ęcclesie sancte crucis de edenesburc  canonicis deo ibi seruientibus ęcclesiam de treuernent cum omnibus iuste ad eam
pertinentibus liberam  ab omni exactione quietam . sicut Walerannus capellanus unquam melius eam habuit  tenuit . salua tenuria ipsius wa
leranni capellani quamdiu in conuersatione seculari manserit . sicut rex Malcolm predictam ęcclesiam de trauernent ecclesie sancte crucis  cano
nicis ibidem deo seruientibus consilio baronum suorum dedit  carta sua confirmauit . Volo itaque  hac presenti carta mea confirmo ut
predicti canonici ecclesiam illam post waleranni dicessum ita libere  quiete obtineant saluo episcopali iure sicut alias suas habent  tenent ele
mo[si]nas suas  possessiones .

His testibus . thoro archidiacono . aiulfo decano . ipso waleranno presente  hoc postulante . Sueino
de fiswic . willelo capellano episcopi . ailuuardo capellano . Iohanne nepote episcopi . Radulfo nepote episcopi .
Robert by the grace of God bishop of St Andrews to all sons of Holy Mother Church Greeting and Blessing:

Let those to come as well as those present know that I have granted in perpetual alms to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there the church of Tranent with everything that rightfully belongs to it, free and quit from every exaction, as Waleran the chaplain ever had and held it well, reserving the tenure of Waleran the chaplain himself for as long as he remains in secular life, just as King Malcolm has given and affirmed by his charter the aforesaid church of Tranent to the church of the Holy Cross and to the canons serving God there by the advice of his barons. I therefore will and by this my charter make firm that after the departure of Waleran the aforesaid canons shall hold that church as freely and quietly as they have and hold their other alms and possessions, reserving the episcopal right.

With these witnesses, Thorald the archdeacon, Aiulf the dean, Waleran himself being present and suggesting it, Swain of Fishwick, William the bishop’s chaplain, Aelward the chaplain, John the bishop’s nephew, Ralph the bishop’s nephew.
